shortlist.title: The spatial method that made the AI render hold the floor plan
shortlist.angle: Build Receipt that fulfills post-008's open promise. Stop asking the model to *style* the plan; hand it coordinates, then verify the output against a plan-coords contract. Show V1 (sofa flipped, dining sealed, invented doors) vs V2 (geometry holds) and the exact check that caught the lie.
shortlist.proof_plan: Screen-record V1 vs V2 renders side by side + the plan-coords.json check that flags the mismatch.

supporting_research.headline: AI renders do not preserve exact proportions; dimensional accuracy not guaranteed
post-008 teased 'the exact spatial method that made the plan stick'

strategic_opinion.reason: Fulfills post-008's open promise; proof (V2 render + plan-coords.json) is ready; pure map-before-build/ship-don't-slide receipt aimed dead-centre at the ICP.
strategic_opinion.timing_note: Needs a 30-min screen-rec of V1/V2 + the coordinate check.

shortlist.title: Only 6% of architects actually use AI. You're not behind — the tools don't fit your Friday.
shortlist.angle: Plain-English Take on 2026 survey data (6% regular use, 8% practice-level despite 85% exposure; 33% blame poor software fit). Reframes the 'you're behind' panic: the gap isn't the owner, it's tools built for the demo not the real workflow. Setu's counter = build to the workflow.
shortlist.proof_plan: Cite the named 2026 surveys + contrast with one screenshot of the workflow-fit system running on a real 3BHK.

supporting_research.headline: Only 6% of architects regularly use AI; 8% of firms at practice level despite 85% exposure
33% cite poor compatibility with current software as the key obstacle

strategic_opinion.reason: On-positioning (extends post-006's 'you're not behind, you're buried' thesis), rides a current survey, and the stat is auditable public data.
strategic_opinion.timing_note: Verify the 6%/8%/33% figures from source before publishing — do not misquote a stat on a brand artefact.

shortlist.title: An AI render in under a minute is easy. A render you can build from is the hard part.
shortlist.angle: Contrarian / honesty post. The market sells one-minute photoreal renders; the dimensions are a polite lie (rooms stretched, doors invented). The trap: a client approves something that can't be built and trust dies on site day. Sequenced AFTER the spatial-method post so it stands on shown proof.
shortlist.proof_plan: Reuse the V1 spatial-failure frames as the 'beautiful but wrong' exhibit; contrast with the verified V2.

supporting_research.headline: AI tools stretch or compress room dimensions interpreting floor plans
AI render in under a minute marketed as turnkey

strategic_opinion.reason: Strong and timely, but overlaps post-008 and is the FIRST Contrarian-pillar post — it must land after the method post (idea-008) so it reads as earned proof, not a repeat complaint.
strategic_opinion.timing_note: Unlocks once idea-008 is published; Contrarian pillar gate (2-3 Build Receipts) already met.

shortlist.title: I built a free AI advisor for design and architecture firms. Ask it anything.
shortlist.angle: Build-in-Public lead magnet, value-first. The live Gemini Gem coaches a firm owner step by step on where AI fits their practice — no sales ask. Standalone dedicated post (post-009 only surfaced it in an auto first-comment).
shortlist.proof_plan: Share the live Gem link + a 20-sec screen-rec of one real owner question answered well.

supporting_research.headline: Gem live at https://da.gd/z52I, already used in post-009 first comment

strategic_opinion.reason: Gem is now live (was the week's blocker); pure give-value, no ask, on-ICP. Mild repeat risk vs the post-009 comment — frame as a standalone utility with a concrete example.
strategic_opinion.timing_note: Value-first; one concrete example question, not a feature list.

shortlist.title: My whole content engine runs on about a cent a day
shortlist.angle: Build-in-Public cost receipt. While Claude Fable 5 lands at $10/$50 per M tokens, the router sends ~90% of work to free/near-free models (gemma $0.00, gpt-4o-mini $0.0001) and saves the expensive model for judgment only. Real model_spend.json numbers.
shortlist.proof_plan: Screenshot model_spend.json: gpt-4o $0.0069, gpt-4o-mini $0.0001, gemma $0.00 — daily total.

supporting_research.headline: Claude Fable 5 GA Jun 9 at $10/$50 per M tokens

strategic_opinion.reason: Positioning leak — it's builder-flex, off the design/construction ICP and drifts toward generic AI-builder content. Real and good, but it doesn't help an architecture owner cross the bridge.
strategic_opinion.when_to_post: after first client (reframe as 'how we keep your automation cheap') or repackage for a builder audience
strategic_opinion.timing_note: Reframe to client value before posting; as-is it's off-ICP.
